codebase: Big Michael - Multi-agent Legal AI Orchestration

vulnerabilities.description: MCP Server exposes all tools without authentication or authorization. Any MCP client can call sensitive operations like submit_task, ingest_document, approve_gate without credentials.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement API key validation or OAuth2 for MCP clients. Add per-tool authorization checks. Require authentication before exposing sensitive tools like approve_gate.

vulnerabilities.description: REST API endpoints (POST /tasks, POST /documents, GET /tasks/:id) have no visible authentication middleware. Unauthenticated users can read all tasks and ingest malicious documents.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Add JWT/API key authentication middleware to all REST endpoints. Implement task-level access control - users should only see their own tasks. Rate limit document ingestion.

vulnerabilities.description: PDF tools invoke Python subprocess (PyMuPDF, Camelot, Tesseract) via src/agents/base.ts tool_use loop. If file paths or arguments aren't properly escaped, command injection is possible when processing untrusted PDFs.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Use subprocess with shell=false and proper argument escaping. Validate file paths against whitelist. Sandbox PDF processing in a separate process. Use security tools to validate argument construction.

vulnerabilities.description: approve_gate and reject_gate endpoints allow any caller to approve/reject findings. A compromised agent or external attacker can force approval of malicious findings without human review.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Require explicit human user ID for gate approvals. Implement rate limiting per human reviewer. Log all gate decisions with user identity. Add secondary approval for high-confidence findings.

vulnerabilities.description: Task memory and knowledge are not properly isolated between tasks. All agents in a round can access the full knowledge store and memory for any task. A compromised agent could exfiltrate data from unrelated tasks.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement task-scoped memory and knowledge access. Query filters in InterRoundMemoryStore.query() must enforce taskId. Create separate Qdrant partitions per task or add row-level security.

vulnerabilities.description: OPENAI_API_KEY defaults to empty string if not set. This silent failure could cause embeddings to fail or fall back to insecure defaults without alerting operators.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Fail startup if required API keys are missing. Use require() for OPENAI_API_KEY instead of optional(). Add health check endpoint that validates all required secrets are present.

vulnerabilities.description: Environment variable names and values may appear in error messages (e.g., 'Missing required env var: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'). If error messages are logged, they expose configuration details to attackers.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Sanitize error messages. Never include env var names in exceptions that reach logs or users. Use numeric error codes instead. Log detailed errors only to secure internal systems.

vulnerabilities.description: Audit log (audit.jsonl) is written to disk with no access control specified. The log contains sensitive information: task descriptions, findings, citations, model calls. File permissions must restrict read access but code doesn't enforce this.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Ensure audit.jsonl is created with 0600 permissions (owner read/write only). Rotate audit logs regularly. Consider encrypting the audit log file. Restrict access via filesystem ACLs.

vulnerabilities.description: ingest() method accepts document content without validation. Malicious documents could contain prompt injection payloads, leading to LLM manipulation when agents search the knowledge store.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Validate and sanitize document content before ingestion. Reject documents containing obvious prompt injection patterns. Log all document ingestion. Implement document source verification.

vulnerabilities.description: System prompts in agent definitions include task descriptions without sanitization. If a task description contains prompt injection, it could manipulate the agent's behavior (e.g., 'Ignore all previous instructions...').
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Never include user input directly in system prompts. Use templated prompts with clear delimiters. Escape user content as data, not code. Validate task descriptions before use.

vulnerabilities.description: OpenAI API key is stored in a module-level variable that persists for the application lifetime. If error handling logs the client object or serializes state, the key could be exposed.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Never serialize or log the OpenAI client. Use object destructuring to avoid logging the full client. Consider using short-lived tokens if OpenAI supports it. Rotate keys regularly.

vulnerabilities.description: Proxy target for dev server is read from process.env.BIG_MICHAEL_API without validation. A malicious environment variable could redirect all frontend API calls to an attacker-controlled server.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Validate proxy target is a safe localhost URL or whitelisted domain. Use URL parsing to prevent SSRF. In production, use hardcoded API URL, not env vars.

vulnerabilities.description: allowedTools array in AgentDefinition controls tool access, but no runtime verification shown. If agent code is compromised, it could call tools outside allowedTools without runtime enforcement.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Add runtime tool access check in src/agents/base.ts runAgenticLoop(). Before calling a tool, verify it's in definition.allowedTools. Log tool access violations.

vulnerabilities.description: Debate and verification protocols call the model with findings + citations. If findings contain injection payloads, the debate/resolver models might be influenced by malicious content.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Validate and sanitize findings before sending to debate/verification. Use structured outputs to prevent injection. Never embed raw finding content in system prompts.

vulnerabilities.description: Logger includes 'meta' objects in output. If sensitive data (API keys, taskIds) is passed in meta, it will be logged to console unencrypted.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement a log filter that sanitizes meta objects. Use logging middleware to mask sensitive keys. Never log API keys, tokens, or PII.

vulnerabilities.description: Interactive terminal detection (process.stdin.isTTY) determines whether to start MCP server. If STDIN is redirected unexpectedly, MCP server might not start or start in wrong mode.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Add explicit MCP_ENABLED env var to control MCP startup. Don't rely on TTY detection. Log which server modes are active at startup.

vulnerabilities.description: Memory entries include agentId and taskId but no tag-based access control shown. Agents might be able to query memory from other agents in the same task.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Add agent-scoped memory queries. Filter by both taskId AND agentId. Implement memory access logs to detect unauthorized access patterns.

vulnerabilities.description: Qdrant API key is optional (process.env.QDRANT_API_KEY). If Qdrant is exposed to network without API key, it's unauthenticated and vulnerable.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Require QDRANT_API_KEY in production. Add validation at startup. Ensure Qdrant only listens on localhost in dev. Use network-level access controls in production.

Authentication & Authorization.details: MCP server and REST API lack authentication. No per-task access control. Gate approvals unprotected.

Injection Vulnerabilities.details: Command injection in PDF tools, prompt injection in task descriptions, no input sanitization in knowledge ingestion.

Data Isolation.details: Task memory and knowledge not isolated. Agents can access unrelated task data.

Secret Management.details: API keys logged accidentally, embedded in errors, optional fallbacks that fail silently.

Audit & Monitoring.details: Audit log unprotected, no access control, may contain PII.

Configuration Security.details: Environment variables without validation, proxy targets not verified, optional secrets fail silently.

topPriorityFixes.issue: Add authentication & authorization to MCP server and REST API
topPriorityFixes.impact: BLOCKS any multi-tenant or public deployment

topPriorityFixes.issue: Implement task-scoped access control for memory, knowledge, and findings
topPriorityFixes.impact: Prevents data leakage between tasks and cross-agent attacks

topPriorityFixes.issue: Secure PDF tool subprocess: escape arguments, validate paths, use shell=false
Sanitize task descriptions and document content before use in prompts
topPriorityFixes.impact: Prevents prompt injection attacks on LLM agents

topPriorityFixes.issue: Protect audit log: restrict file permissions, encrypt, redact sensitive fields
topPriorityFixes.impact: Prevents information disclosure via log access

complianceGaps: No multi-tenancy support (all users share all data)
No audit trail for sensitive operations
No encryption at rest for sensitive data
No rate limiting on API endpoints
recommendations: Implement OAuth2 or API key authentication for all entry points (MCP, REST)
Add per-task authorization - users should only access their own tasks
Validate all user input before use in prompts or tool calls
Implement runtime tool access control - verify tools against allowedTools whitelist
Sanitize document content before ingestion - scan for prompt injection patterns
Use parameterized queries in all database interactions (Qdrant filters)
Escape subprocess arguments with shell=false, use allowlist for file paths
Protect audit log with file-level ACLs (0600 permissions), rotate logs
Add request signing/verification for gate approvals - require human user context
Implement data segregation - task-scoped knowledge, memory, and agent context
Add health check endpoint that validates all secrets are properly loaded
Implement rate limiting per user/task to prevent abuse
Use security headers in REST API (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options)
Add CORS protection - whitelist allowed origins, don't use wildcard
Log all sensitive operations (document ingest, gate approval, finding creation)
Implement secret rotation for API keys
Add input validation layer - maximum document sizes, content type checks
Use environment variable validation at startup - fail fast on missing secrets
Implement agent behavior monitoring - detect anomalous tool usage patterns
Add cryptographic signing to findings before they enter the debate protocol
